007.title: execute_task returns inside _merge_lock causing lock held during cost recording
007.failure_reason: Gemini retry failed: Gemini CLI exited with code 1

hypotheses.claim: Lock/context-manager refactoring tasks have a higher failure rate than other task types because the orchestrated prompts tend to be longer and contain deeply nested indented code that either exceeds size limits or contains CMD-breaking characters
evidence: task-007 failed with Gemini CLI exit code 1 on a lock/return refactoring task
The orchestrator prompt already had special guidance for lock changes but it was insufficient to prevent the failure
hypotheses.suggested_action: Switch lock refactoring tasks to pure English description format instead of Find/Replace blocks, keeping prompts under 2000 characters

hypotheses.claim: The executor.py file is the most frequently modified file in the codebase and accumulates merge conflicts or stale-base issues when multiple tasks target it in the same session
evidence: Task 001 specifically fixed stale-base issues after squash-merge
hypotheses.suggested_action: The planner should limit concurrent tasks targeting the same file to reduce conflict risk, or serialize tasks that touch executor.py

hypotheses.claim: pyrefly type checking errors for rich.console and rich.panel are persistent false positives caused by the validation environment not having the rich package installed, and should not block merges
evidence: All 6 completed tasks showed identical pyrefly missing-import errors for rich.console and rich.panel
All were correctly treated as non-fatal
hypotheses.suggested_action: Consider adding rich to the pyrefly environment or suppressing these specific import errors in validation
